decisions.title: Commit to Explore V2 as default experience by Q2 2026
decisions.context: ENG-9729 styling Legacy button, ENG-9728 updating CTA, ENG-9698 maintaining V1 sharelinks. Signals show we're straddling two experiences. Need clear migration timeline to 'Nail the Core' on V2.

arguments.for: Clean migration path increases V2 adoption, reduces maintenance burden, focuses eng on moat-building features. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar.
arguments.against: Risk alienating power users dependent on V1 features. May need to delay if V2 parity incomplete. Current signals suggest transition in progress but not complete.

decisions.title: Prioritize SiftStream production readiness over new features
decisions.context: ENG-9646 error handling, ENG-9647 configuration, ENG-9653 debugging capabilities. Three concurrent tickets suggest SiftStream needs operational maturity. Critical for 'Embed Deeper' pillar.

arguments.for: SiftStream is foundation for edge deployment and real-time decisioning. Poor reliability blocks 'Sift Edge' product rollout and enterprise adoption.
arguments.against: May slow feature velocity short-term. Could defer revenue-generating Canvas/Python Rules improvements. But operational excellence is non-negotiable for enterprise.

decisions.title: Accelerate infrastructure-as-code deployment standardization
decisions.context: ENG-9172, ENG-9173, ENG-9176, ENG-9165 show raw deployment work. ENG-9722 ephemeral environments and ENG-9658 auto-redeploy suggest modern DevEx push. Need to decide: custom vs. platform approach.
decisions.blockedBy: Cloud cost analysis incomplete. Security team review of ENG-9706 (security group findings) needed before architecture lock-in.

arguments.for: Standardized infra accelerates 'Sift Edge' deployment to customer sites. Ephemeral envs improve dev velocity 40%+. Builds operational moat.
arguments.against: Significant eng investment (~2 quarters). May distract from product features. But delay risks technical debt explosion as customer count grows.

decisions.title: Address calculated channel reliability blockers immediately
decisions.context: ENG-9151 (max/min fail >5 args), ENG-9681 (saved calc fails to plot), ENG-9703 (contextual channels overwritten). Core calculation engine showing fragility. Blocks 'Decisions Captured' metric.

arguments.for: Calculated channels are primary mechanism users create decision logic. Bugs directly reduce North Star metric. High-severity user friction in Canvas/Python Rules.
arguments.against: May seem like maintenance vs. innovation. But reliability IS the moat. Users won't adopt Ask SIFT if underlying calc engine is unreliable.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Meeting load 33% above sustainable threshold. Recommend blocking Fri for strategic thinking on infrastructure decisions.
alerts: Three 1:1s today suggest team needs unblocking time
No focus blocks >90min this week - limits deep architecture work
Four decisions pending with April deadlines - need acceleration meeting
